title: Roasted Ethiopian Gouda Cheese Gomen
description: Crisp, roasted Ethiopian dish featuring melted Gouda cheese, served over a nimono-simmered vegetable base, perfect for a romantic date night. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is easy to make and requires minimal ingredients.
instructions: Cut the onion, ginger, and garlic into small pieces and roast in the oven for 10 minutes.
Peel and chop the carrots and potatoes into bite-sized pieces and roast in the oven for 10 minutes.
Add the spinach to the roasted vegetables and stir until wilted.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat and sauté the roasted vegetables for 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, allspice, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Crumble the Gouda cheese into the pan and stir until melted and well combined.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the cheese mixture over the nimono-simmered vegetable base, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Simmer the chopped onion, ginger, and garlic in vegetable broth until the vegetables are tender and the liquid has reduced.
Strain the broth and combine it with the roasted vegetables, spinach, carrots, and potatoes.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the flavors have melded together and the liquid has reduced slightly.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the gomen over the nimono-simmered vegetable base, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
